title: Horse And Man
content:
When the Horse first took Man on his back,
    To help him the Stag to attack;
    How little his dread,
    As the enemy fled,
    Man would make him his slave & his hack.

    Advantages May Be Dearly Bought
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Crane, titled "Horse And Man", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
